descriptions.description: The 96L6E Cheese Board is an all-altitude acquisition and detection radar capable of operating independently or as part of the S-400 Triumf or S-300PMU-2 air-defense systems. Developed by Almaz-Antey, the 96L6E provides high and low-altitude target detection, classification, and initial track initiation across extended ranges. The radar can function as a command post for S-300 or S-400 battalions, making it a versatile component in layered air-defense architecture. Its interoperability with both modern and legacy systems reflects the evolutionary nature of Russian air-defense network development.
descriptions.shortDescription: Dual-system all-altitude acquisition radar for S-400 and S-300PMU-2 air-defense systems.

descriptions.description: The 96L6E employs a long-range phased-array antenna optimized for low and high-altitude detection through multiple beam steering patterns and frequency-agile pulse compression. The radar uses advanced clutter rejection and electronic counter-countermeasures (ECCM) techniques to maintain performance in dense jamming environments. The signal processor integrates target classification, track formation, and command-post functionality with network-centric data distribution. The 96L6-1 variant serves as the command system for S-400 and S-500 batteries, enabling distributed fire-unit coordination through automated track correlation and tactical network interfaces. The modular architecture permits rapid deployment and reconfiguration for force rotation and tactical redeployment.
descriptions.shortDescription: Long-range phased-array radar with command-post and independent deployment capability.
